generation.image_prompt: Mystical 1900s lithography tarot card illustration. Vintage woodcut texture, limited earthy color palette. Clean bold black ink outlines. NO BORDERS. FULL BLEED IMAGE. WIDE FIELD OF VIEW. ANTHROPOMORPHIC: One noble Taiwan leopard cat STRICTLY STANDING UPRIGHT ON TWO LEGS, riding atop a large friendly wild dog (or walking in a procession). The cat holds a single wooden wand FIRMLY WITH ITS PAWS, adorned with a laurel wreath of green leaves. Other leopard cats cheer in the background. Slender and agile feline build. High-fidelity biological markers: narrow small-cat facial structure, distinct white stripes from nose to forehead, white muzzle, and large white spots behind black ears. No baked-in text.
generation.narrative: A leopard cat returns in triumph, symbolizing a successful restoration effort that has become a beacon of hope for the public.

meanings.upright: Victory, public recognition, success in conservation, hope, and reaching a milestone.
meanings.reversed: Lack of recognition, delayed success, or a hollow victory that lacks ecological depth.

ecology.mapping_note: Six of Wands is the 'Conservation Success Story'—the individual that becomes the face of the movement and thrives despite all odds, educating the public about the power of recovery.

website.excerpt: A triumphant return, celebrating the hard-won victories of habitat restoration and public awareness.
website.seo_description: Six of Wands reimagined as a conservation success story and the hope of restoration.

meaning.zh: 勝利、公眾認可、復育成功、希望，以及達成里程碑的時刻。
meaning.en: Victory, public recognition, success in conservation, hope, and reaching a milestone.
